articles.title: EL SALVADOR SPLITS BITCOIN ACROSS 14 WALLETS TO REDUCE QUANTUM RISK
articles.summary: El Salvador has transferred its entire Bitcoin reserve of 6,274 BTC ($678 million) from a single wallet into 14 separate addresses, with each new wallet holding up to 500 BTC. The Central American nation implemented this security measure as a precaution against potential future quantum computing threats, which could theoretically crack Bitcoin's elliptic curve cryptography and compromise funds stored in addresses with exposed public keys. While quantum computing currently poses no immediate threat to Bitcoin security, existing quantum computers are unable to crack even basic encryption keys. Distributing funds across multiple addresses would minimize the impact of any potential quantum attack, as public keys are only revealed after BTC is spent.

articles.title: STRATEGY'S PREFERRED SHARES FORM A BULLISH CIRCLE AROUND BITCOIN
articles.summary: Strategy raised $5.6 billion year-to-date through four preferred stock offerings (STRK, STRF, STRD, and STRC), representing 12% of all U.S. IPO issuance in 2025 and cementing its position as the largest corporate Bitcoin holder with 632,457 BTC. Performance across the preferred shares has diverged significantly, with STRF leading at 31% returns, STRK at 19%, STRC at 8%, while STRD posted a negative 6% return. Despite the company's aggressive Bitcoin accumulation strategy, MSTR shares have underperformed Bitcoin itself, gaining 13% year-to-date compared to Bitcoin's 18% rise, as the stock trades at a 1.60x multiple to its Bitcoin net asset value.

articles.title: BETH: ETHEREUM'S PROOF OF BURN TOKEN
articles.summary: Ethereum's new BETH token represents provably burned ETH, formalizing scarcity and making destruction auditable onchain. Users can burn ETH via a contract and receive BETH 1:1, turning Ethereum's burn mechanic into a transferable asset. This opens up new design space for DeFi, governance, and financial instruments built around proof of burn.

articles.title: ETHEREUM FOUNDATION AIMS TO MAKE LAYER 2S FEEL LIKE ONE CHAIN
articles.summary: The Ethereum Foundation has unveiled a roadmap to address Layer 2 fragmentation through three workstreams: Initialisation, Acceleration, and Finalisation, aimed at making cross-L2 activity feel seamless. The initiative introduces an Open Intents Framework and Ethereum Interoperability Layer (EIL) to enable trustless cross-chain transactions, while targeting faster L1 confirmations of 15-30 seconds and exploring potential slot time reductions from 12 to 6 seconds. Key deliverables include production contracts for the intents framework by Q4 2025, broad availability of fast confirmation rules by Q1 2026, and ongoing research into zero-knowledge proof integration for real-time settlement.

articles.title: AI REVENUE ENGINES: THE GATEWAY TO BROADER ADOPTION
articles.summary: Decentralized AI (DeAI) protocols are beginning to generate revenue through compute, data, and agent services. Multiple DeAI projects across categories, including fine-tuned models, DePIN infrastructure, portfolio management, and agent launchpads, are demonstrating product-market fit with users willing to pay for services, mirroring how centralized AI companies reach $30 million in revenue 5x faster than traditional SaaS. Despite AI being the most disruptive technology of the current era, AI tokens represent only 1.9% of the total altcoin market cap, suggesting significant undervaluation relative to their revenue-generating potential.

articles.title: THE 2025 CRYPTO CARD COMPARISON
articles.summary: The crypto card market is evolving beyond simple prepaid wrappers toward solutions that leverage crypto's core advantages, with new offerings in 2025 focusing on four key differentiators: custody, yield generation, multi-chain flexibility, and privacy. EtherFi Cash leads the DeFi-native approach by allowing users to spend directly from yield-generating vaults – earning 9-12% on USDC while maintaining 3%+ cashback – while PAYY prioritizes privacy through zero-knowledge settlement, and Cypher enables spending across 500+ tokens on 18+ chains. Traditional players like Coinbase are also entering with mainstream credit card offerings featuring crypto rewards.

articles.title: CFTC, ETFS, AND THE SHAPE OF THIS BULL
articles.summary: The current bull market will be driven by ETFs, centralized exchanges, and digital asset treasuries. Retail enthusiasm for onchain “gambling” like memecoins and NFTs has faded. DeFi will benefit if ETF investors push into onchain financial infrastructure while broader crypto adoption shifts toward payments, global settlement of USD/RWAs, and BTC/ETH as stores of value.

articles.title: ETHEREUM AS THE FUTURE OF FINANCE
articles.summary: Wall Street will inevitably migrate to Ethereum rails, staking, running validators, and using DeFi to replace siloed legacy infrastructure. ETH will overtake BTC and ultimately become the base layer of global finance as decentralized trust is the ultimate commodity.

articles.title: ETHEREUM STABLECOIN SUPPLY HITS $160B
articles.summary: Stablecoin supply on Ethereum has reached an all-time high of over $160 billion, more than doubling since January 2024.

articles.title: STRATEGY'S BITCOIN HOLDINGS EXCEEDS SOVEREIGNS
articles.summary: The top six sovereign Bitcoin holders in 2025 (altogether ~527k BTC with the U.S. at ~207k BTC, China at ~194k, and smaller allocations from the UK, Ukraine, Bhutan, and El Salvador) have less Bitcoin than Strategy (632K+ BTC).

articles.title: SOLANA MOBILE'S SEED VAULT UX
articles.summary: Solana Mobile's Seed Vault turns a phone into a hardware wallet secured by biometrics, offering a major usability and security upgrade.

articles.title: KALSHI FLIPPED POLYMARKET OPEN INTEREST
articles.summary: Kalshi became the leading prediction market by open interest in the first weekend of the US college football season, likely supported by its status as the main regulated prediction market in the United States.
